<!-- Copyright © 2023-2026 ValidMind Inc. All rights reserved.
Refer to the LICENSE file in the root of this repository for details.
SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0 AND ValidMind Commercial -->

::: {.panel-tabset}

#### Create document from {{< var vm.product >}} template

a. Under **Create Document**, select **From {{< var vm.product >}} Template**.

b. Select your desired **Template** from the drop-down menu.

c. Click **Use Template** to apply the template.

#### Upload existing PDF file

Convert a Portable Document Format `.pdf` file (PDF) into an editable document in the {{< var validmind.platform >}}, allowing you to seamlessly work with existing PDFs as you would with any other template-based document, such as adding content blocks and attaching test results.

{{< var vm.product >}} automatically extracts the structure and content from your PDF, generates a document template that maps to the extracted sections, and populates each section in the template with the contents from your original document.^[**PDF conversion runs in the background.** Depending on the size and complexity of your PDF, conversion can take anywhere from a few seconds to several minutes. You can continue working in the {{< var validmind.platform >}} while the conversion runs.]

To upload a PDF:

a. Under **Create Document**, select **From PDF Upload**.

b. Drag and drop a `.pdf` file into the upload area, or click **choose a file** to select from your file explorer.^[Only one PDF can be uploaded per document. To change your selection, click **Remove** to clear the currently queued file before choosing a new one.]

c. Choose to:

    - **Upload as Static Document** — The uploaded PDF is stored as a static file. You can view it within the {{< var validmind.platform >}} but you cannot modify its structure or contents.
    - **Upload & Convert to Editable Document** — Track conversion progress in the banner that appears at the top of the document page. When conversion completes, the page automatically refreshes to display your document as an editable template with the content extracted from the PDF.

        If you need to stop an in-progress PDF conversion:

        i. In the conversion progress banner that appears at the top of the document page, click **Cancel**.
        ii. On the Cancel File Conversion modal, optionally toggle **Delete file from this document** on to also remove the uploaded PDF file.^[If you chose to keep the file, the PDF remains attached to the document as a static file. You can view the PDF but it will not be converted into an editable document.]
        iii. Click **Cancel Conversion** to confirm.

::: {.callout-button .pl4 .nt4}
::: {.callout collapse="true" appearance="minimal"}

### How do I get the best results when converting PDFs into editable documents?

- **Use text-based PDFs** — PDFs that contain selectable text convert most reliably. Scanned image-only PDFs may produce poor or empty results.
- **Use clear heading hierarchy** — Documents with well-defined headings (H1, H2, H3) produce more accurate section mappings in the generated document template.
- **Keep formatting simple** — Standard paragraphs, lists, and tables convert well. Complex layouts such as multi-column text, sidebars, or heavily nested tables may not be preserved accurately.

::: {.embed-tip}
### How can I trust that the conversion is accurate?

Always review the generated template after conversion completes to verify that sections and content were extracted correctly, and make any necessary adjustments.
:::

<br>

## Troubleshooting

Conversion fails immediately
: - The file may be corrupted or password-protected.
- Verify the PDF opens correctly in a local PDF viewer. Remove any password protection before uploading.

Conversion completes but sections are empty
: - The PDF may contain scanned images instead of text.
- Use OCR software to convert the scanned PDF to a text-based PDF before uploading.

Sections are mapped incorrectly
: - The PDF may lack consistent heading styles.
- Review the PDF structure and ensure headings are formatted distinctly from body text. After conversion, manually adjust section content as needed.

Conversion takes too long
: - The PDF may be very large or contain complex elements.
- Try uploading a simplified version of the PDF. Remove large embedded images or unnecessary appendices.

PDF upload completes but conversion fails
: - An unexpected error occurred during processing.
- Delete the document and recreate it, then re-upload the PDF file and try the conversion again.

::: {.embed-tip}
### My PDF conversion is stuck. What can I do?

If a conversion appears to be stuck, click **Cancel** in the conversion progress banner to stop processing and try again with a revised PDF.
:::

<br>

:::
:::

:::
